1.1: PAUL, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and brother Ti-mo’the-us, unto Phi-le’mon our dearly beloved and our fellow-worker,
1.2: And to our beloved Ap’phi-a and Archip’pus our fellow-labourer, and to the congregation in your house:
1.3: Grace be with you, and peace, from God our Father and our LORD Jesus Christ.
1.4: I thank my God, and always make mention of you in my prayers,
1.5: Since I have heard of your faith and love, which you have toward our LORD Jesus, and toward all saints;
1.6: That the participation of your faith may bear fruits in works, and in knowledge of everything that is good which you have in Jesus Christ.
1.7: For we have great joy and consolation in your love, and the hearts of the saints are refreshed.
1.8: For this reason, I have great boldness in Christ, to command of you those things which are right,
1.9: And for love’s sake I earnestly beseech you; even I, Paul, an old man as you know, and now also a prisoner for the sake of Jesus Christ.
1.10: I beseech you on behalf of my son Ones’i- mus, whom I converted during my imprisonment:
1.11: But of whom in the past you could not make use, but now he is very useful both to you and to me.
1.12: I send him to you again: welcome him as my own boy:
1.13: For I would have kept him with me to minister to me in your place during my imprisonment for the gospel:
1.14: But I did not wish to do anything without consulting you, that your good deeds might not be done as though by compulsion, but of your own desire.
1.15: Perhaps this was the reason why he left you for a while, that you can now engage him for ever;
1.16: Henceforth not as a servant, but more than a servant, a brother beloved, specially to me, and much more to you, both in the flesh and in our LORD.
1.17: Now, therefore, if you still count me a partner, welcome him as you would me.
1.18: And if he has caused you any loss, or if he owes you anything, put it on my account:
1.19: I, Paul, have written this with my own hand; I will repay it, not reminding you that you owe to me even your own life.
1.20: Indeed, my brother, let me have comfort through you in our LORD: refresh my heart in Christ.
1.21: Because I have confidence in your obedience, I wrote to you, knowing that you will also do more than I ask.
1.22: In addition to all this, prepare me a lodging; for I hope that through your prayers I shall be spared to come to you.
1.23: Ep’a-phras, my fellow prisoner in Jesus Christ, salutes you;
1.24: So do Mark, Ar-is-tar’chus, De’mas, and Luke, my fellow-workers.
1.25: The grace of our LORD Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
